control_description: The entity restricts physical access to facilities and protected information assets to meet the entity's objectives.

evidence_files.description: Helios Systems physical security policy defining badge access controls, CCTV requirements, and termination procedures
Badge access log for Q4 2025 with 55 entries showing employee badge swipes across all access points

prompt: Review the physical security policy and badge access log for Helios Systems. Assess whether the organization is meeting its SOC 2 CC6.4 obligations for restricting physical access to facilities. Cross-reference the access log against the policy requirements to identify any compliance gaps.

findings.title: Terminated employee badge used 2 days after termination date
findings.description: Badge access log row 47 shows employee Rachel Simmons (badge ID B-2847) accessing the General Office zone on November 17, 2025, at 08:42 AM. The badge access log employee_status column shows her status as 'Terminated' with a termination_date of November 15, 2025. This means a terminated employee's badge was used to access the facility 2 days after separation. Policy Section 4.3 requires badge deactivation within 24 hours of termination notification. This indicates a failure in the badge deactivation process — the badge should have been deactivated on or before November 16. A terminated employee retaining physical access creates risk of unauthorized data access, equipment theft, or sabotage.
findings.expected_reasoning: The auditor should review all badge swipes and check the employee_status field for any non-Active entries. Row 47 shows Rachel Simmons with status 'Terminated' and termination_date November 15, but a badge swipe on November 17. The policy requires deactivation within 24 hours. This is a 2-day gap indicating the badge was never deactivated as required.

findings.title: CCTV retention configured at 30 days but policy requires 90 days
findings.description: Policy Section 5.3 specifies that CCTV footage must be retained for a minimum of 90 days. However, the policy's Appendix A configuration table shows the CCTV NVR retention setting is configured at 30 days. This means footage is overwritten after 30 days, leaving a 60-day gap where footage required by policy is unavailable. If a security incident is discovered more than 30 days after it occurred, the relevant CCTV footage will have been deleted despite the policy requiring 90-day availability.
findings.expected_reasoning: The auditor should note that Section 5.3 requires 90-day CCTV retention. Appendix A shows the actual NVR configuration with a 30-day retention setting. The mismatch between the policy requirement and the configuration table indicates a control implementation gap. The 30-day setting is only one-third of the required retention period.
